title: CLAUDE.md
text: R package (v0.9.0) downloading processing Brazilian public education data INEP, FNDE, STN, CAPES. Provides 14+ get_*() functions fetch, cache, extract, return tidy data frames. text output (column names, CLI messages) Portuguese.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Check package (full R CMD check) Rscript -e "devtools::check()"  # Run all tests Rscript -e "devtools::test()"  # Run a single test file Rscript -e "devtools::test(filter = 'utils-cache')"  # Regenerate documentation (roxygen2 → man/ + NAMESPACE) Rscript -e "devtools::document()"  # Load package for interactive testing Rscript -e "devtools::load_all()"  # Build pkgdown site Rscript -e "pkgdown::build_site()"

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Key internal modules: - R/utils-cache.R — Local cache system (tempdir()/educabR_cache user-configured). Functions: cache_path(), is_cached(), get_cache_dir(), set_cache_dir(), clear_cache(), list_cache() - R/utils-download.R — HTTP downloads retry (3 attempts, 600s timeout), archive extraction (ZIP/7z/RAR), URL construction, dynamic year discovery via HEAD requests - R/utils-validation.R — Per-dataset validators checking column counts, expected names, non-empty results - R/zzz.R — Package init, reads educabR.cache_dir option
code: validate parameters → check cache → download if needed → extract archive → read file → standardize column names → transform → validate output → return tibble

title: CLAUDE.md
text: datasets follow pattern: - CSV--ZIP (): ENEM, SAEB, Censo Escolar, Censo Superior, ENADE, ENCCEJA - Excel (IDEB, FUNDEB distribution, CAPES): Use readxl, multi-sheet logic - OData API (FUNDEB enrollment): httr2 JSON requests FNDE API - ENEM 2024+: Split “participantes” (demographics) “resultados” (scores) — two separate files

title: educabR
text: Read English O educabR dá acesso direto aos principais dados educacionais públicos Brasil — censo escolar e exames nacionais indicadores universitários e financiamento da educação — tudo de dentro R. Sem downloads manuais, sem navegar portais governo: basta escolher o dataset, o ano, e receber uma tabela limpa, pronta para análise. O pacote cobre 14 conjuntos de dados publicados pelo INEP, FNDE, CAPES e STN, abrangendo educação básica, educação superior, pós-graduação e financiamento via FUNDEB.

title: educabR
text: Mapa IDEB por estado em poucas linhas:
code: # instalar pacote "pacman" se não estiver instalado if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")  # instalar e/ou carregar pacotes p_load(   educabR,   geobr,   tidyverse )  # ler dados do IDEB ideb <- get_ideb(   level  = "estado",    stage  = "anos_iniciais",    metric = "indicador",    year   = 2023   )  # ler dados espaciais states <- read_state(year = 2020, showProgress = FALSE)  # plotar dados states |>   left_join(ideb, by = c("abbrev_state" = "uf_sigla")) |>   drop_na() |>   filter(rede == "Pública" & indicador == "IDEB") |>   ggplot() +   geom_sf(aes(fill = valor), color = "white", size = .2) +   scale_fill_distiller(palette = "YlGn", direction = 1, name = "IDEB") +   labs(     title    = "IDEB - rede pública de ensino - 2023",     subtitle = "Anos iniciais (1º - 4º) por estado"   ) +   theme_void()

title: How to compare CPC indicators between public and private institutions?
text: CPC (Conceito Preliminar de Curso) quality indicator undergraduate courses, ranging 1 (lowest) 5 (highest). Courses scoring 1 2 flagged -site evaluation.
code: cpc <- get_cpc(year = 2023) glimpse(cpc) #> Rows: 9,812 #> Columns: 39 #> $ ano                      <dbl> 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, … #> $ codigo_da_ies            <dbl> 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 2, … #> $ nome_da_ies              <chr> "UNIVERSIDADE FEDERAL DE MATO G… #> $ sigla_da_ies             <chr> "UFMT", "UFMT", "UFMT", "UFMT"… #> $ categoria_administrativa <chr> "Pública Federal", "Pública Fed… #> $ codigo_do_curso          <dbl> 3, 9, 10, 12, 16, 17, 20, 37, … #> $ area_de_avaliacao        <chr> "ENGENHARIA CIVIL", "AGRONOMIA"… #> $ sigla_da_uf              <chr> "MT", "MT", "MT", "MT", "MT", "… #> $ cpc_continuo             <dbl> 3.429, 3.482, 3.064, 2.792, 4.… #> $ cpc_faixa                <dbl> 4, 4, 4, 3, 5, 4, 4, 4, 4, 5, … #> # ℹ 29 more variables

title: How to compare CPC indicators between public and private institutions?
text: categoria_administrativa column classifies institutions. exact column name coding may vary year – check names(cpc) downloading.
code: cpc_classified <-   cpc |>   mutate(     sector = case_when(       categoria_administrativa %in% c(         "Publica Federal", "Publica Estadual", "Publica Municipal",         "P\u00fablica Federal", "P\u00fablica Estadual", "P\u00fablica Municipal"       ) ~ "Public",       .default = "Private"     )   )

title: How to compare CPC indicators between public and private institutions?
code: cpc_classified |>   filter(!is.na(cpc_faixa)) |>   count(sector, cpc_faixa) |>   mutate(pct = n / sum(n) * 100, .by = sector) |>   ggplot(aes(x = factor(cpc_faixa), y = pct, fill = sector)) +   geom_col(position = "dodge") +   labs(     title = "CPC Score Distribution: Public vs Private (2023)",     x     = "CPC Score (1-5)",     y     = "Percentage of Courses (%)",     fill  = "Sector"   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Average CPC by sector and knowledge area
title: How to compare CPC indicators between public and private institutions?
code: cpc_classified |>   filter(!is.na(cpc_continuo), !is.na(area_de_avaliacao)) |>   summarise(     mean_cpc = mean(cpc_continuo, na.rm = TRUE),     n = n(),     .by = c(sector, area_de_avaliacao)   ) |>   filter(n >= 10) |>   pivot_wider(     names_from  = sector,     values_from = c(mean_cpc, n)   ) |>   mutate(gap = mean_cpc_Public - mean_cpc_Private) |>   slice_max(abs(gap), n = 15) |>   ggplot(aes(x = reorder(area_de_avaliacao, gap), y = gap)) +   geom_col(aes(fill = gap > 0)) +   coord_flip() +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("TRUE" = "#2a9d8f", "FALSE" = "#e76f51"),     labels = c("TRUE" = "Public higher", "FALSE" = "Private higher")   ) +   labs(     title = "CPC Gap: Public minus Private, by Knowledge Area (2023)",     x     = NULL,     y     = "CPC difference (public - private)",     fill  = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

what: Combining with IGC for institutional view
title: How to compare CPC indicators between public and private institutions?
text: IGC (Indice Geral de Cursos) provides institution-level quality score. Combining CPC IGC gives course-level institution-level perspective.
code: igc <- get_igc(year = 2023)  igc |>   mutate(     sector = case_when(       categoria_administrativa %in% c(         "Publica Federal", "Publica Estadual", "Publica Municipal",         "P\u00fablica Federal", "P\u00fablica Estadual", "P\u00fablica Municipal"       ) ~ "Public",       .default = "Private"     )   ) |>   filter(!is.na(igc_continuo)) |>   ggplot(aes(x = sector, y = igc_continuo, fill = sector)) +   geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Public" = "#2a9d8f", "Private" = "#e76f51")) +   labs(     title = "IGC Distribution: Public vs Private (2023)",     x     = NULL,     y     = "IGC (Continuous)"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Education Funding: FUNDEB
text: can combine datasets analyze per-student funding across states.
code: # Total transfers by state dist <- get_fundeb_distribution(2018) transfers <-    dist |>   group_by(uf) |>   summarise(total_transfer = sum(valor, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")  # Total enrollment by state mat <- get_fundeb_enrollment(2018) enrollment <-    mat |>   group_by(uf) |>   summarise(total_students = sum(qtd_matricula, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")  # Per-student funding funding <-    inner_join(transfers, enrollment, by = "uf") |>   mutate(per_student = total_transfer / total_students) |>   arrange(desc(per_student))  ggplot(funding, aes(x = reorder(uf, per_student), y = per_student)) +   geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "FUNDEB Per-Student Funding by State (2018)",     x     = NULL,     y     = "R$ per Student"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::number_format(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ","))

what: Downloading ENEM data for multiple years
title: How has ENEM performance evolved by state?
text: ENEM microdata files large (1-3 GB ), use n_max work samples. full analysis, remove n_max parameter.
code: # WARNING: each year downloads ~600 MB from INEP. This may take several minutes. years <- c(2019, 2021, 2022, 2023)  enem <-   years |>   purrr::map(\(y) get_enem(year = y, n_max = 50000) |> mutate(year = y)) |>   bind_rows()

what: Average math score by state and year
title: How has ENEM performance evolved by state?
code: scores_by_state <-   enem |>   filter(!is.na(nu_nota_mt), !is.na(sg_uf_prova)) |>   summarise(     mean_math = mean(nu_nota_mt, na.rm = TRUE),     n = n(),     .by = c(sg_uf_prova, year)   )  # Top 5 states in the most recent year top_states <-   scores_by_state |>   filter(year == max(year)) |>   slice_max(mean_math, n = 5) |>   pull(sg_uf_prova)  scores_by_state |>   filter(sg_uf_prova %in% top_states) |>   ggplot(aes(x = factor(year), y = mean_math, color = sg_uf_prova, group = sg_uf_prova)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   labs(     title = "ENEM Math Score Evolution - Top 5 States",     x     = "Year",     y     = "Average Math Score",     color = "State"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: How has ENEM performance evolved by state?
code: region_map <- c(   AC = "North",     AP = "North",     AM = "North",     PA = "North",   RO = "North",     RR = "North",     TO = "North",   AL = "Northeast", BA = "Northeast", CE = "Northeast",   MA = "Northeast", PB = "Northeast", PE = "Northeast",   PI = "Northeast", RN = "Northeast", SE = "Northeast",   DF = "Midwest",   GO = "Midwest",   MT = "Midwest",   MS = "Midwest",   ES = "Southeast", MG = "Southeast", RJ = "Southeast", SP = "Southeast",   PR = "South",     RS = "South",     SC = "South" )  enem |>   filter(!is.na(nu_nota_mt), !is.na(sg_uf_prova)) |>   mutate(region = region_map[sg_uf_prova]) |>   summarise(     mean_math = mean(nu_nota_mt, na.rm = TRUE),     .by = c(region, year)   ) |>   ggplot(aes(x = factor(year), y = mean_math, color = region, group = region)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   labs(     title = "ENEM Math Score by Region",     x     = "Year",     y     = "Average Math Score",     color = "Region"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: How has ENEM performance evolved by state?
code: enem |>   filter(!is.na(sg_uf_prova)) |>   summarise(     math       = mean(nu_nota_mt, na.rm = TRUE),     languages  = mean(nu_nota_lc, na.rm = TRUE),     humanities = mean(nu_nota_ch, na.rm = TRUE),     sciences   = mean(nu_nota_cn, na.rm = TRUE),     essay      = mean(nu_nota_redacao, na.rm = TRUE),     .by = year   ) |>   pivot_longer(-year, names_to = "subject", values_to = "mean_score") |>   ggplot(aes(x = factor(year), y = mean_score, color = subject, group = subject)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   labs(     title = "National Average ENEM Scores by Subject",     x     = "Year",     y     = "Average Score",     color = "Subject"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Getting Started with educabR
text: educabR caches downloaded files avoid repeated downloads. default, uses temporary directory. persist data across sessions:
code: # Check current cache directory get_cache_dir()  # Set a permanent directory set_cache_dir("~/educabR_data")  # List cached files list_cache()  # Clear cache (when needed) clear_cache()

title: Getting Started with educabR
text: IDEB main indicator basic education quality Brazil, combining test performance (SAEB) grade promotion rates.

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # See available combinations list_ideb_available()

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # IDEB by school - Early elementary (1st-5th grade) ideb_schools <- get_ideb(   level  = "escola",   stage  = "anos_iniciais",   metric = "indicador",   year   = 2021 )  # IDEB by municipality - High school (1st-4th grade) ideb_municipalities <- get_ideb(   level  = "municipio",   stage  = "ensino_medio",   metric = "indicador",   year   = 2023 )  # Filter by state after downloading ideb_sp <-   ideb_schools |>   dplyr::filter(uf_sigla == "SP")

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # View structure glimpse(ideb_schools) #> Rows: 194,715 #> Columns: 9 #> $ uf_sigla         <chr> "RO", "RO", "RO", ... #> $ municipio_codigo <chr> "1100015", "1100015", ... #> $ municipio_nome   <chr> "Alta Floresta D'Oeste", ... #> $ escola_id        <chr> "11024372", "11024666", ... #> $ escola_nome      <chr> "EMEIEF ANA NERY", "EMEIEF BOA ESPERANCA", ... #> $ rede             <chr> "Municipal", "Municipal", ... #> $ ano              <int> 2021, 2021, ... #> $ indicador        <chr> "IDEB", "IDEB", ... #> $ valor            <dbl> NA, NA, 5.9, 4.1, ...

what: Example analysis: Average IDEB by state
title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # Calculate average IDEB by state (observed indicator only) ideb_by_state <-   ideb_schools |>   filter(indicador == "IDEB", !is.na(valor)) |>   group_by(uf_sigla) |>   summarise(     n_schools   = n(),     mean_ideb   = mean(valor, na.rm = TRUE),     median_ideb = median(valor, na.rm = TRUE)   ) |>   arrange(desc(mean_ideb))  # Plot ggplot(ideb_by_state, aes(x = reorder(uf_sigla, mean_ideb), y = mean_ideb)) +   geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "Average IDEB by State - Early Elementary (2021)",     x     = "State",     y     = "Average IDEB"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Getting Started with educabR
code: # Download historical series (get_ideb() already returns long format) ideb_history <- get_ideb(   level  = "municipio",   stage  = "anos_iniciais",   metric = "indicador",   year   = c(2017, 2019, 2021, 2023) )  # National trend trend <-   ideb_history |>   filter(indicador == "IDEB") |>   group_by(ano) |>   summarise(mean_ideb = mean(valor, na.rm = TRUE))  ggplot(trend, aes(x = factor(ano), y = mean_ideb)) +   geom_col(fill = "darkgreen", width = 0.6) +   geom_text(     aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", mean_ideb)),     vjust = -0.5, size = 3.5   ) +   labs(     title = "IDEB Trend - Early Elementary",     x     = "Year",     y     = "National Average IDEB"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1)))

title: Higher and Graduate Education: Census, ENADE, IDD, CPC, IGC, and CAPES
text: CPC, IGC, IDD, ENADE closely related. example combine comprehensive view.
code: # Load CPC and IGC for the same year cpc <- get_cpc(2023) igc <- get_igc(2023)  # Compare institution-level quality # IGC gives the overall institution score # CPC gives individual course scores within each institution igc_summary <-    igc |>   filter(!is.na(igc_faixa)) |>   select(codigo_da_ies, sigla_da_ies, igc_continuo, igc_faixa)  cpc_summary <-    cpc |>   filter(!is.na(cpc_continuo)) |>   group_by(codigo_da_ies) |>   summarise(     n_courses = n(),     mean_cpc  = mean(cpc_continuo, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups   = "drop"   )  combined <- inner_join(igc_summary, cpc_summary, by = "codigo_da_ies")  ggplot(combined, aes(x = mean_cpc, y = igc_continuo, size = n_courses)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.4, color = "steelblue") +   labs(     title = "IGC vs Average CPC by Institution (2023)",     x     = "Average CPC (Continuous)",     y     = "IGC (Continuous)",     size  = "Courses Evaluated"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Which municipalities improved the most in IDEB?
text: get_ideb() returns data long format available editions. single call year = c(2005, 2023) gives us two endpoints need comparison.
code: ideb <- get_ideb(   level  = "municipio",   stage  = "anos_iniciais",   metric = "indicador",   year   = c(2005, 2023) ) glimpse(ideb) #> Rows: 86,982 #> Columns: 7 #> $ uf_sigla         <chr> "RO", "RO", "RO", "RO", "RO", "RO", … #> $ municipio_codigo <chr> "1100015", "1100015", "1100015", "1100… #> $ municipio_nome   <chr> "Alta Floresta D'Oeste", "Alta Florest… #> $ rede             <chr> "Estadual", "Municipal", "Pública", "E… #> $ ano              <int> 2005, 2005, 2005, 2005, 2005, 2005, 20… #> $ indicador        <chr> "IDEB", "IDEB", "IDEB", "IDEB", "IDEB… #> $ valor            <dbl> 3.5, NA, 3.7, 4.0, 3.5, 3.7, 4.1, 3.4…

title: Which municipalities improved the most in IDEB?
text: filter IDEB indicator municipal schools, pivot wide format get one column per year, compute change.
code: ideb_change <-   ideb |>   filter(indicador == "IDEB") |>   filter(rede == "Municipal") |>   select(uf_sigla, municipio_codigo, municipio_nome, ano, valor) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = ano, values_from = valor, names_prefix = "ideb_") |>   filter(!is.na(ideb_2005), !is.na(ideb_2023)) |>   mutate(     change     = ideb_2023 - ideb_2005,     pct_change = change / ideb_2005 * 100   )

title: Which municipalities improved the most in IDEB?
code: ideb_change |>   slice_max(change, n = 20) |>   ggplot(aes(     x = reorder(paste(municipio_nome, uf_sigla, sep = " - "), change),     y = change   )) +   geom_col(fill = "#2a9d8f") +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title    = "Top 20 Municipalities by IDEB Improvement (2005-2023)",     subtitle = "Municipal schools, early elementary (1st-5th grade)",     x        = NULL,     y        = "IDEB change (2023 - 2005)"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Which municipalities improved the most in IDEB?
code: ideb_change |>   ggplot(aes(x = reorder(uf_sigla, change, FUN = median), y = change)) +   geom_boxplot(fill = "#2a9d8f", alpha = 0.6, outlier.size = 0.5) +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title    = "IDEB Improvement Distribution by State (2005-2023)",     subtitle = "Municipal schools, early elementary (1st-5th grade)",     x        = NULL,     y        = "IDEB change"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Which municipalities improved the most in IDEB?
code: # Pick the top 5 improvers top5 <-   ideb_change |>   filter(rede == "Municipal") |>   slice_max(change, n = 5) |>   pull(municipio_codigo)  # Get full history for these municipalities ideb_full <-   get_ideb(     level  = "municipio",     stage  = "anos_iniciais",     metric = "indicador"   ) |>   filter(rede == "Municipal")  ideb_full |>   filter(municipio_codigo %in% top5, indicador == "IDEB") |>   mutate(label = paste(municipio_nome, uf_sigla, sep = " - ")) |>   ggplot(aes(x = factor(ano), y = valor, color = label, group = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   labs(     title    = "IDEB Trajectory - Top 5 Improvers",     subtitle = "Municipal schools, early elementary (1st-5th grade)",     x        = "Year",     y     = "IDEB",     color = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Which municipalities improved the most in IDEB?
code: ideb_change |>   filter(change < 0) |>   slice_min(change, n = 15) |>   ggplot(aes(     x = reorder(paste(municipio_nome, uf_sigla, sep = " - "), change),     y = change   )) +   geom_col(fill = "#e76f51") +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title    = "Municipalities with Largest IDEB Decline (2005-2023)",     subtitle = "Municipal schools, early elementary (1st-5th grade)",     x        = NULL,     y        = "IDEB change (2023 - 2005)"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Mapping education indicators with geobr
text: simplest map uses state-level data. download IDEB scores join state geometries geobr.
code: ideb_uf <-   get_ideb(level = "estado", stage = "anos_iniciais", metric = "indicador", year = 2023) |>   filter(rede == "Total", indicador == "IDEB")  states <- read_state(year = 2020, showProgress = FALSE)  states |>   left_join(ideb_uf, by = c("abbrev_state" = "uf_sigla")) |>   ggplot() +   geom_sf(aes(fill = valor), color = "white", linewidth = .2) +   scale_fill_distiller(palette = "YlGn", direction = 1, name = "IDEB") +   labs(title = "IDEB 2023 — Early elementary by state") +   theme_void()

title: Mapping education indicators with geobr
text: Municipality-level maps reveal within-state inequality state averages hide. municipio_codigo column educabR uses 7-digit IBGE code, matches code_muni geobr.
code: ideb_muni <- get_ideb(   level  = "municipio",   stage  = "anos_iniciais",   metric = "indicador",   year   = 2023 )  # Keep only public schools and the IDEB indicator ideb_muni <-   ideb_muni |>   filter(grepl("blica", rede), indicador == "IDEB")  municipalities <- read_municipality(year = 2020, showProgress = FALSE) municipalities |>   mutate(code_muni = as.character(code_muni)) |>   left_join(ideb_muni, by = c("code_muni" = "municipio_codigo")) |>   ggplot() +   geom_sf(aes(fill = valor), color = NA) +   scale_fill_distiller(palette = "YlGn", direction = 1, name = "IDEB") +   labs(title = "IDEB 2023 — Early elementary by municipality (public schools)") +   theme_void()

title: Mapping education indicators with geobr
text: closer look, filter datasets single state. map IDEB across municipalities Minas Gerais.
code: ideb_mg <-   ideb_muni |>   filter(uf_sigla == "MG")  munis_mg <- read_municipality(code_muni = "MG", year = 2020, showProgress = FALSE)  munis_mg |>   mutate(code_muni = as.character(code_muni)) |>   left_join(ideb_mg, by = c("code_muni" = "municipio_codigo")) |>   ggplot() +   geom_sf(aes(fill = valor), color = "grey90", linewidth = .1) +   scale_fill_distiller(palette = "YlGn", direction = 1, name = "IDEB") +   labs(title = "IDEB 2023 — Early elementary in Minas Gerais") +   theme_void()

title: Mapping education indicators with geobr
text: Side--side maps make easy visualize regional progress. download two editions use facets.
code: ideb_time <-   get_ideb(     level  = "estado",     stage  = "anos_iniciais",     metric = "indicador",     year   = c(2017, 2023)   ) |>   filter(rede == "Total", indicador == "IDEB")  states |>   left_join(ideb_time, by = c("abbrev_state" = "uf_sigla")) |>   ggplot() +   geom_sf(aes(fill = valor), color = "white", linewidth = .2) +   scale_fill_distiller(palette = "YlGn", direction = 1, name = "IDEB") +   facet_wrap(~ano, strip.position = "bottom") +   labs(title = "IDEB evolution — Early elementary (2017 vs 2023)") +   theme_void() +   theme(     legend.position = "bottom",     plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),     strip.text = element_text(size = 11, margin = margin(t = 5))   )

title: What does school infrastructure look like across Brazil?
text: School Census contains one row per school (~217,000 schools 2023) 400 variables covering infrastructure, staffing, programs. Note: full national file 30 MB compressed. filtering state, rows read filtering, first call may take moment.
code: # Download all schools for 2023 escolas <- get_censo_escolar(year = 2023)  # Or filter by state for faster exploration escolas_sp <- get_censo_escolar(year = 2023, uf = "SP")

title: What does school infrastructure look like across Brazil?
text: School Census uses binary columns (1 = yes, 0 = ) infrastructure item. Let us compute percentage schools resource nationwide.
code: indicators <- c(   "in_internet",   "in_banda_larga",   "in_biblioteca",   "in_laboratorio_informatica",   "in_laboratorio_ciencias",   "in_quadra_esportes",   "in_agua_potavel",   "in_esgoto_rede_publica" )  infra_summary <-   escolas |>   summarise(across(all_of(indicators), ~ mean(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)) |>   pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "indicator", values_to = "pct") |>   mutate(     label = c(       "Internet", "Broadband", "Library", "Computer lab",       "Science lab", "Sports court", "Drinking water", "Public sewage"     )   )  ggplot(infra_summary, aes(x = reorder(label, pct), y = pct)) +   geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "Percentage of Schools with Key Infrastructure (2023)",     x     = NULL,     y     = "% of schools"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: What does school infrastructure look like across Brazil?
text: Federal, state, municipal, private schools different resource levels. tp_dependencia column encodes administrative type.
code: admin_labels <- c(   "1" = "Federal",   "2" = "State",   "3" = "Municipal",   "4" = "Private" )  infra_admin <-   escolas |>   mutate(admin = admin_labels[as.character(tp_dependencia)]) |>   group_by(admin) |>   summarise(     Internet       = mean(in_internet == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     Library        = mean(in_biblioteca == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     `Computer lab` = mean(in_laboratorio_informatica == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     `Science lab`  = mean(in_laboratorio_ciencias == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   pivot_longer(-admin, names_to = "resource", values_to = "pct")  ggplot(infra_admin, aes(x = resource, y = pct, fill = admin)) +   geom_col(position = "dodge") +   labs(     title = "School Infrastructure by Administrative Type (2023)",     x     = NULL,     y     = "% of schools",     fill  = "Type"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

title: What does school infrastructure look like across Brazil?
text: Northern Northeastern states typically fewer resources South Southeast. Grouping region reveals gap.
code: region_labels <- c(   "Norte"        = "North",   "Nordeste"     = "Northeast",   "Sudeste"      = "Southeast",   "Sul"          = "South",   "Centro-Oeste" = "Central-West" )  infra_region <-   escolas |>   mutate(region = region_labels[no_regiao]) |>   group_by(region) |>   summarise(     Internet       = mean(in_internet == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     Library        = mean(in_biblioteca == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     `Science lab`  = mean(in_laboratorio_ciencias == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     `Sports court` = mean(in_quadra_esportes == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   pivot_longer(-region, names_to = "resource", values_to = "pct")  ggplot(infra_region, aes(x = region, y = pct, fill = resource)) +   geom_col(position = "dodge") +   labs(     title = "School Infrastructure by Region (2023)",     x     = NULL,     y     = "% of schools",     fill  = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: What does school infrastructure look like across Brazil?
text: tp_localizacao column distinguishes urban (1) rural (2) schools. infrastructure gap one starkest Brazilian education.
code: infra_location <-   escolas |>   mutate(     location = ifelse(tp_localizacao == 1, "Urban", "Rural")   ) |>   group_by(location) |>   summarise(     Internet       = mean(in_internet == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     Broadband      = mean(in_banda_larga == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     Library        = mean(in_biblioteca == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     `Computer lab` = mean(in_laboratorio_informatica == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     `Science lab`  = mean(in_laboratorio_ciencias == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     `Sports court` = mean(in_quadra_esportes == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   pivot_longer(-location, names_to = "resource", values_to = "pct")  ggplot(infra_location, aes(x = resource, y = pct, fill = location)) +   geom_col(position = "dodge") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Urban" = "steelblue", "Rural" = "coral")) +   labs(     title = "School Infrastructure: Urban vs Rural (2023)",     x     = NULL,     y     = "% of schools",     fill  = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

title: What does school infrastructure look like across Brazil?
text: School Census tracks specific accessibility features. in_acessibilidade_inexistente flag marks schools accessibility .
code: access_cols <- c(   "in_acessibilidade_rampas",   "in_acessibilidade_corrimao",   "in_acessibilidade_elevador",   "in_acessibilidade_pisos_tateis",   "in_acessibilidade_sinal_sonoro",   "in_acessibilidade_sinal_tatil",   "in_acessibilidade_sinal_visual",   "in_acessibilidade_inexistente" )  access_labels <- c(   "Ramps", "Handrails", "Elevator", "Tactile floors",   "Sound signals", "Tactile signals", "Visual signals", "None" )  access_summary <-   escolas |>   summarise(across(all_of(access_cols), ~ mean(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)) |>   pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "feature", values_to = "pct") |>   mutate(label = access_labels)  ggplot(access_summary, aes(x = reorder(label, pct), y = pct)) +   geom_col(fill = "#2a9d8f") +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "School Accessibility Features (2023)",     x     = NULL,     y     = "% of schools"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: What does school infrastructure look like across Brazil?
text: per-state view highlights states lagging behind digital connectivity.
code: internet_uf <-   escolas |>   group_by(sg_uf) |>   summarise(     pct_internet = mean(in_internet == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   arrange(pct_internet)  ggplot(internet_uf, aes(x = reorder(sg_uf, pct_internet), y = pct_internet)) +   geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "Schools with Internet Access by State (2023)",     x     = NULL,     y     = "% of schools"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Download and Process Brazilian Education Data from INEP
text: Leia em Português educabR gives direct access Brazil’s main public education datasets — school census national exams university indicators education funding — within R. manual downloads, navigating government portals: just pick dataset, choose year, get clean, analysis-ready table. package covers 14 datasets published INEP, FNDE, CAPES, STN, spanning basic education, higher education, graduate programs, FUNDEB funding.

what: educabR: Download and Process Brazilian Education Data from INEP — educabR-package
title: educabR: Download and Process Brazilian Education Data from INEP — educabR-package
text: Download process public education data INEP (Instituto Nacional de Estudos e Pesquisas Educacionais Anísio Teixeira). Provides functions access microdata School Census (Censo Escolar), ENEM (Exame Nacional Ensino Médio), SAEB (Sistema de Avaliação da Educação Básica), Higher Education Census (Censo da Educação Superior), ENADE (Exame Nacional de Desempenho dos Estudantes), ENCCEJA (Exame Nacional para Certificação de Competências de Jovens e Adultos), IDD (Indicador de Diferença entre os Desempenhos Observado e Esperado), CPC (Conceito Preliminar de Curso), IGC (Índice Geral de Cursos), CAPES graduate education data, FUNDEB (Fundo de Manutencao e Desenvolvimento da Educacao Basica), IDEB (Índice de Desenvolvimento da Educação Básica), educational datasets. Returns data tidy format ready analysis. Data source: INEP Open Data Portal https://www.gov.br/inep/pt-br/acesso--informacao/dados-abertos. educabR package provides functions download process public education data INEP (Instituto Nacional de Estudos e Pesquisas Educacionais Anísio Teixeira). offers easy access microdata : School Census (Censo Escolar): Annual data schools, enrollment, teachers, classes basic education ENEM: Data National High School Exam SAEB: Basic Education Assessment System (student performance) Higher Education Census (Censo da Educação Superior): Annual data institutions, courses, students, faculty higher education ENADE: National Student Performance Exam (higher education) IDD: Value-added indicator undergraduate courses CPC: Preliminary Course Concept (undergraduate quality indicator) IGC: General Index Courses (institutional quality indicator) CAPES: Graduate education data (programs, students, faculty) ENCCEJA: Youth Adult Education Certification Exam IDEB: Basic Education Development Index functions return data tidy format, ready analysis tidyverse tools.

text: year year census (2009-2024). type Type data load. Options: "ies": Higher education institutions (default) "cursos": Undergraduate courses "alunos": Student enrollment "docentes": Faculty/professors uf Optional. title: Get Higher Education Census (Censo da Educação Superior) data — get_censo_superior
text: Higher Education Census comprehensive statistical survey higher education institutions (HEIs) Brazil. collects data HEIs offering undergraduate graduate programs. Data types: "ies": One row per institution — administrative data, location, academic organization, funding type. "cursos": One row per undergraduate course — area study, modality (-person/distance), enrollment counts. "alunos": One row per student enrollment — demographics, program, admission type, enrollment status. "docentes": One row per faculty member — education level, employment type, teaching regime. text: year year data (2007-2026). uf Optional. UF code (e.g., "SP", "RJ") filter state. Default NULL (states). source Optional. funding source filter . One : "FPE", "FPM", "IPI", "ITR", "VAAF", "VAAT", "VAAR", "ICMS", "IPVA", "ITCMD". Default NULL (sources). destination Optional. transfer destination. title: Get FUNDEB distribution data — get_fundeb_distribution
text: tibble tidy (long) format columns: estados State name uf State code (UF) mes_ano Date (last day month) origem Funding source (FPE, FPM, ICMS, etc.) destino Transfer destination ("UF" "Municipio") tabela Table type ("Fundeb" "Ajuste Fundeb") valor Transfer amount BRL (numeric)

title: Get FUNDEB distribution data — get_fundeb_distribution
text: FUNDEB (Fundo de Manutencao e Desenvolvimento da Educacao Basica e de Valorizacao dos Profissionais da Educacao) main funding mechanism basic education Brazil. Excel file STN contains ~20 data sheets named prefix indicating destination (E_ states, M_ municipalities) suffix indicating funding source (e.g., E_FPE, M_ICMS). sheet contains two tables: main FUNDEB transfers FUNDEB adjustment table. Important notes: Data sourced STN (Tesouro Nacional), INEP. Files Excel format (XLS) — requires readxl package. Column names standardized lowercase underscores. Summary sheets (Resumo, Total, etc.) automatically excluded.

title: educabR 1.0.0
text: get_ideb() new signature: get_ideb(level, stage, metric, year, quiet). old positional usage get_ideb(year, level, stage) still works deprecation warning, year parameter now filters IDEB editions instead selecting file download. get_ideb() now returns data tidy long format instead wide format. Output columns depend metric parameter ("indicador", "aprovacao", "nota", "meta"). get_ideb() now supports 5 geographic levels: "escola", "municipio", "estado", "regiao", "brasil" (previously escola municipio). get_ideb() always downloads recent IDEB file available, contains full historical series. year parameter filters editions. get_ideb_series() deprecated. Use get_ideb(level, stage, metric) instead. list_ideb_available() now returns level, stage, metric columns (previously returned year, level, stage). uf parameter removed get_ideb(). Filter result dplyr::filter() instead.

title: educabR 0.9.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-03

title: educabR 0.9.0
text: available_years() now dynamically discovers available years querying data sources (HEAD requests INEP, OData queries FNDE). Results cached per session. Falls back hardcoded list offline. available_years() now accepts "fundeb_enrollment" separate dataset name. Previously, "fundeb" shared distribution enrollment. Code columns (CO_*, CD_*) now read character instead numeric across datasets. prevents loss leading zeros codes like municipality codes, course codes, institution codes.

title: educabR 0.9.0
text: Fixed get_enade() failing 9 19 available years. INEP uses inconsistent URLs ENADE: _LGPD suffix 2012-2019, .rar format 2022. Added hardcoded URL map (enade_urls) 19 correct URLs. Fixed get_fundeb_enrollment() accepting years data FNDE API. API currently data 2017-2018. Fixed encoding warning (“unable translate MARCO”) Windows caused Unicode cedilla FUNDEB month name map. Fixed clear_cache() failing delete files Windows memory-mapped readr. Now deletes entire directories warns locked files. Fixed ZIP extraction fallback triggering ENADE 2017 (“Illegal byte sequence” error matched encoding detection pattern).

title: educabR 0.9.0
text: Added .rar archive extraction support via 7-Zip. find_7z() searches common Windows install paths 7z PATH. Added strip_diacriticals() internal helper encoding-safe text matching. read_inep_file() now auto-detects code columns (CO_*, CD_*) file header reads character. user action required. read_ideb_excel() read_excel_safe() (CPC/IGC) now convert code columns character reading.

what: FUNDEB (Fundo de Manutencao e Desenvolvimento da Educacao Basica)
title: educabR 0.8.0
text: get_fundeb_distribution(): Download FUNDEB resource distribution data (years 2007-2026). Reads sheets STN Excel files returns tidy long-format data monthly transfer amounts state, funding source, destination (states/municipalities), table type (fundeb/adjustment). get_fundeb_enrollment(): Download FUNDEB enrollment data. Fetches FNDE OData API automatic pagination. Results cached CSV. Filtering parameters distribution: uf, source (FPE, FPM, ICMS, etc.), destination (“uf” “municipio”). Data sources: Tesouro Transparente (https://www.tesourotransparente.gov.br) FNDE (https://www.fnde.gov.br).

title: educabR 0.7.0
text: get_capes(): Download CAPES graduate education data (years 2013-2024). Supports 5 data types: programs ("programas"), students ("discentes"), faculty ("docentes"), courses ("cursos"), theses/dissertations catalog ("catalogo"). Uses CKAN API dynamically discover download URLs (CAPES URLs contain UUIDs). Data source: CAPES Open Data Portal (https://dadosabertos.capes.gov.br).

title: educabR 0.6.0

title: educabR 0.6.0

text: Fixed encoding detection Windows using iconv() instead validEnc(). Fixed "Latin-1" encoding name "latin1" Windows codepage compatibility. Fixed ENEM 2024+ support: new type parameter split files ("participantes", "resultados"). Added SAS datetime parsing Censo Escolar date columns (dt_*). Converted IDEB vl_* columns character numeric, handling "-", "ND", comma decimals.
